title: NOC 22213 – Land Survey Technologists and Technicians | GTR Immigration
description: Land Survey Technologists and Technicians are in strong demand across Canada, driven by large-scale infrastructure projects, residential development, transportation corridors, resource mapping, and smart-city planning initiatives. Classified under NOC 22213 (TEER 2), this occupation aligns well with:

classification.teer: 2 – College diploma or apprenticeship normally required

duties: Assist survey engineers in developing field methods
Conduct surveys using electronic instruments, GPS & UAVs
Help prepare maps and technical drawings
employment_requirements: Two- to three-year college diploma in geomatics or land-survey technology (technologists)
One- to two-year college diploma in geomatics or land-survey technology (technicians)
In Quebec, regulatory-body membership required for the title Professional Technologist
job_outlook: This competitive outlook still favours candidates with:
additional_information: Canadian employers continue to recruit land-survey professionals for:
Engineering & consulting firmsArchitectural practicesMunicipal & provincial governmentsInfrastructure authoritiesMining & energy projectsPrivate geomatics companies
Annual salaries typically range between $36,600–$84,700 CAD, depending on province, licensing, and field-survey specialization.
